title: Smoky Chewy Irish Beef Stew
description: A hearty, smoky Irish stew with a sweet apricot jam finish and bhuna spices, delivering chewy beef and an energizing flavor profile.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat. Brown the beef cubes in batches, then set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the remaining oil. Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, bhuna masala, smoked paprika, black pepper, and kosher salt. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the browned beef to the pot and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Stir in the apricot jam, allowing it to melt and coat the beef and vegetables.
Add sliced carrots, cubed potatoes, and pour in the beef broth. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes, stirring occasionally until the beef is chewy yet tender.
If the stew becomes too thick, add a splash of water or extra broth. Check seasoning and adjust salt or pepper as needed.
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley or a drizzle of olive oil.
